cards.name: Visa M - Inversionista
cards.subhead: Current official investor path for qualifying foreign investment.
cards.thresholdCop: COP 612,816,750 reference using the current 2026 minimum wage
cards.thresholdUsd: About USD 155,100 using CasaClaro's verified FX rate
cards.summary: Used for foreign direct investment maintained in Colombian real estate or other approved investment channels, with funds registered correctly through the banking system.
mustKnow: Official Cancilleria page reviewed March 29, 2026 and last updated March 18, 2026.
Requires proof that the qualifying investment has been made and maintained.
Requires a health policy covering all risks in Colombia.
Do not skip foreign investment registration if you want the visa strategy to remain clean.

cards.name: Visa M - Pensionado
cards.subhead: Current official retirement path for applicants with qualifying pension income.
cards.thresholdLabel: Monthly pension of at least 3 monthly minimum wages
cards.thresholdCop: COP 5,252,715 per month using the current 2026 minimum wage
cards.thresholdUsd: About USD 1,330 per month using CasaClaro's verified FX rate
cards.summary: Built for retirees who can document pension income from a recognized government or private source and want a long-stay option in Colombia.
mustKnow: Official Cancilleria page reviewed March 29, 2026 and last updated March 18, 2026.
Requires a pension certificate showing the recurring payment.
Requires a health policy covering all risks in Colombia.
The official page notes this category does not by itself grant affiliation to the Colombian social security health system except where bilateral or multilateral agreements apply.

cards.name: Visa V - Nomada Digital
cards.subhead: Useful for remote earners who are not yet buying at investor-visa scale.
cards.thresholdCop: See official requirements because this path is tied to document type and income proof rather than a real estate threshold.
cards.summary: Relevant for remote workers testing Colombia before a purchase, especially those deciding between Medellin, Pereira, Bucaramanga, or coastal living.
mustKnow: Best used as a transition path, not a substitute for proper purchase due diligence.
The visa decision still requires clean supporting documents and private health coverage.

healthcare.summary: Healthcare is one of the first practical questions expatriates and retirees ask, and it should be. In practice, many foreigners begin with private insurance, prepaid medicine, or international coverage while they confirm longer-term local eligibility with counsel and an insurer.
highlights: Medellin and Bogota offer the deepest private specialist networks and bilingual support.
Bucaramanga, Cali, and Pereira are strong mid-sized options for long-stay living with reputable private care.
Coastal cities can work well for routine care, but complex treatment planning may still point you inland depending on your needs.
healthcare.sourceLabel: MinSalud affiliation and protection in health overview
healthcare.summaryEs: La salud es una de las primeras preguntas prácticas de expatriados, jubilados y familias locales. En la práctica, muchas personas combinan seguro privado, medicina prepagada o cobertura internacional mientras ajustan su estrategia de largo plazo con asesoría profesional.
highlightsEs: Medellín y Bogotá concentran la red privada más amplia de especialistas y soporte bilingüe.
Bucaramanga, Cali y Pereira son opciones sólidas de ciudad intermedia para vida de larga estancia con atención privada confiable.
En ciudades costeras, los casos complejos pueden requerir planeación adicional o traslados a hubs médicos del interior según necesidad clínica.

cities.name: Medellin
cities.shortTagline: Spring weather, digital-nomad demand, and resilient hillside neighborhoods.
cities.climate: Mild spring weather year-round with little temperature shock.
cities.pace: Lively, social, and easier to navigate than Bogota if you choose the right district.

walkability.label: High in selected districts
walkability.summary: Laureles, parts of El Poblado, and Envigado work well on foot, though hillside geography still matters.
walkability.summaryEs: La caminabilidad depende del barrio, topografía y acceso a servicios de rutina.

healthcare.label: Top-tier private care
healthcare.summary: One of Colombia's strongest private healthcare markets, with major clinics, specialists, and health-tourism demand.
healthcare.summaryEs: La cobertura médica varía por ciudad y corredor urbano; confirma red privada útil para tu perfil.

destinationHighlights: Cafe culture, mountain views, and year-round outdoor living
Weekend access to Guatape and the wider Antioquia region
Strong coworking and relocation support ecosystem
watchouts: Do not assume nightly rentals are legal in every building.
Micro-location matters more than citywide averages.
Some towers carry heavy HOA fees that can eat into gross yield.
cities.historicalContext: Medellin evolved from an industrial capital into one of Latin America's most studied urban transformation stories, with public transit, innovation districts, and strong neighborhood identities shaping its modern appeal.
cities.investorAppeal: Investors target Medellin for diversified rental demand from local professionals, remote workers, students, and health tourism, especially in established zones with strong services and walkability.
cities.lifestyleHighlights: Expats value the temperate climate, strong cafe culture, modern private healthcare, coworking scene, and weekend access to the broader Antioquia region.
introText: Medellin combines mountain views, spring-like weather, and a mature urban fabric that appeals to both end users and income-focused investors. Neighborhood performance varies sharply, so block-level due diligence matters more here than headline city averages.

The city remains one of Colombia's most recognized destinations for foreigners, which supports leasing demand but also means buyers must stay disciplined on pricing and compliance. Poblado captures global attention, while Laureles, Envigado, and select emerging corridors can offer better value-adjusted entry points.

For expatriates, Medellin offers the strongest ecosystem of bilingual services, private clinics, coworking spaces, and lifestyle amenities outside Bogota. That makes it a practical first landing point, but also a competitive market where understanding local regulations is essential.

regulations.airbnbSummary: Short-term rentals are widely offered, but compliant operation depends on building bylaws, local registration requirements, and tourism-use permissions. Many buildings in El Poblado and Laureles now restrict nightly rentals through HOA rules.
regulations.airbnbDetails: Buyers should verify the building's property regime, tourism registration status, and whether the apartment can legally be marketed for stays under 30 days. In practice, enforcement risk is highest when owners assume city demand overrides building rules.
regulations.airbnbSummaryEs: La corta estancia depende de reglamento del edificio y cumplimiento regulatorio local; no se debe asumir por demanda.
regulations.airbnbDetailsEs: Antes de comprar, valida licencias, régimen de propiedad horizontal y política de administración para evitar fricciones operativas.

cities.foreignOwnership: Foreign buyers can generally own urban property in Colombia without nationality-based restrictions. Standard compliance includes registering inbound investment through the banking system; special restrictions are more relevant near national borders or protected coastal zones than within Medellin's urban neighborhoods.
cities.mortgageNote: Foreigners may obtain local financing in some cases, typically with 40% to 60% loan-to-value depending on income documentation and bank policy.

rentalMarket.tenantMix: Remote workers, returning Colombians, local professionals, health-tourism families, and long-stay expats.

rentalMarket.furnishedDemand: Strong in El Poblado, Laureles, and premium serviced corridors.

cities.taglineEs: City of Eternal Spring en Colombia
cities.shortTaglineEs: Medellin: mercado activo, lectura por barrios y decisiones con datos locales.

cities.historicalContextEs: Medellin evolucionó con identidad regional propia y hoy combina capas históricas, servicios urbanos y submercados residenciales con comportamientos distintos por zona.
cities.investorAppealEs: El atractivo inversionista de Medellin se apoya en demanda de arriendo segmentada, profundidad operativa y posibilidad de entrar por barrio con disciplina de precio por m2.
cities.lifestyleHighlightsEs: Compradores y familias valoran en Medellin la combinación entre servicios, conectividad, salud y estilo de vida, siempre que la selección de barrio sea precisa.
introTextEs: Medellin no se entiende solo con promedios de ciudad. La lectura correcta empieza por microzonas, caminabilidad real y calidad de servicios cotidianos.
Para compradores internacionales, locales y brokers, este mercado premia la disciplina en precio por m2, revisión de reglamentos y análisis de costos de mantenimiento antes de negociar.
La decisión más fuerte en Medellin suele venir de combinar narrativa de vida diaria con números: salud, movilidad, carga de reparaciones y demanda de arriendo por barrio.
destinationHighlightsEs: Escena local y ritmo urbano con identidad propia.
Submercados por barrio con perfiles de comprador diferentes.
Conectividad y servicios que impactan la calidad de vida diaria.

Alinea estrategia legal, migratoria y tributaria antes de cerrar.

cities.historicalContext: Bogota is Colombia's political and financial center, layered with colonial history, major universities, and a dense concentration of corporate and diplomatic activity.
cities.investorAppeal: Investors favor Bogota for depth of market, liquidity, and more stable long-term leasing demand across executive, student, and family segments.
cities.lifestyleHighlights: Expats gain access to top hospitals, international schools, diverse dining, and nonstop flight connectivity, balanced against traffic and cooler weather.
introText: Bogota is the country's largest and most liquid residential market, which makes it attractive for investors who prioritize scale and year-round occupancy over resort-style positioning. Demand is driven by professional relocation, university enrollment, embassies, healthcare, and domestic migration.

The city rewards submarket selection. Prime northern districts can preserve capital and support executive rentals, while emerging areas may offer better yield if transportation, safety, and tenant profile line up.

For expatriates, Bogota is often the most functional base in Colombia. It offers the broadest service ecosystem and strongest international connectivity, but buyers should underwrite time-to-commute, micro-location walkability, and building age carefully.

regulations.airbnbSummary: Short-term rentals exist in Bogota, but condominium rules and local licensing requirements materially affect legality and operating risk. Professional operators usually focus on buildings already structured for hospitality use.
regulations.airbnbDetails: Before purchase, confirm tourism registration requirements, HOA restrictions, and whether the building has a track record of allowing transient occupancy. A residential zoning assumption alone is not enough.
regulations.airbnbSummaryEs: La corta estancia depende de reglamento del edificio y cumplimiento regulatorio local; no se debe asumir por demanda.
regulations.airbnbDetailsEs: Antes de comprar, valida licencias, régimen de propiedad horizontal y política de administración para evitar fricciones operativas.

cities.foreignOwnership: Urban property ownership is generally open to foreigners on the same basis as Colombian nationals. Buyers should ensure purchase funds are properly registered and conduct title review through local counsel.
cities.mortgageNote: Local bank financing is possible but usually document-heavy for non-residents, with conservative leverage compared with U.S. standards.

introText: Cartagena is Colombia's most internationally legible leisure market. Buyers are typically underwriting either tourism cash flow, lifestyle usage with partial rental offset, or long-term capital preservation in historically limited neighborhoods.

The market can be highly profitable when licensing, HOA permissions, and property operations are aligned, but it is unforgiving when those assumptions are wrong. Coastal wear, management quality, and building rules have a direct impact on net returns.

For expatriates and second-home owners, Cartagena offers beach proximity, strong hospitality infrastructure, and an active cultural calendar. The tradeoff is that entry pricing and operating complexity are generally higher than inland Colombian cities.

introText: Cali can offer a more favorable entry point than Medellin, Bogota, or Cartagena, which makes it relevant for investors targeting cash flow and room for neighborhood-driven appreciation. Pricing dispersion is meaningful, so working with local comparables matters.

Demand tends to be steadier in zones anchored by healthcare, universities, and established residential amenities. Trophy pricing is less common, which can help disciplined buyers avoid overpaying for narrative rather than fundamentals.

For expatriates, Cali offers a lower-cost urban lifestyle, strong food and dance culture, and access to the Pacific region. The city requires a practical approach to mobility and safety, with hyperlocal due diligence doing most of the risk work.

introText: Barranquilla is often overlooked by first-time foreign buyers, but it has developed into a credible investment market with business-driven housing demand and improving upper-income residential product. It tends to be more functional than glamorous, which can be a strength for underwriting stability.

The north of the city captures most premium demand, while broader metropolitan growth supports a wide middle-income tenant base. Investors focused on steady occupancy rather than tourism narrative may find the city compelling.

For expatriates, Barranquilla offers a practical Caribbean lifestyle with lower profile and less volatility than Cartagena. The key is selecting neighborhoods with strong infrastructure, flood resilience, and building quality.

cities.historicalContext: Santa Marta is one of South America's oldest cities, combining Caribbean waterfront living with access to Tayrona, mountain ecosystems, and a growing domestic tourism base.

introText: Santa Marta sits in a useful middle ground for foreign buyers who want Caribbean exposure without Cartagena's top-tier pricing. That can create attractive yield potential, especially where the asset is operationally suited for tourism or hybrid personal-use strategies.

The city is not uniform. Beachfront projects, El Rodadero towers, and more local residential pockets serve different tenant and guest profiles, and performance depends heavily on management quality and building rules.

For expatriates and seasonal residents, Santa Marta offers a more relaxed setting with strong access to beaches and outdoor destinations. Buyers should prioritize project quality, water reliability, and neighborhood-level infrastructure before stretching on headline sea-view pricing.

introText: Pereira offers a strong information advantage because it remains less saturated by foreign capital than Medellin or Cartagena while still benefiting from a recognizable regional identity. Buyers often find a more rational relationship between pricing and local incomes here.

For expatriates, Pereira is appealing as a softer landing into Colombia. It delivers manageable city scale, a comfortable climate, and access to countryside living without giving up urban basics.

cities.investorAppeal: Investors like Bucaramanga for steady local demand, lower speculative pressure, and quality-of-life fundamentals that support owner-occupier stability.
cities.lifestyleHighlights: Expats find a manageable city, spring-like weather, good medical services, and easier daily logistics than larger metros.
introText: Bucaramanga is not the loudest market in Colombia, which is precisely why some investors like it. The city tends to be driven by local end users, professionals, and families rather than hype cycles, which can make pricing more disciplined.

Yield expectations are usually moderate rather than explosive, but the city can suit buyers who want a livable environment and less tourism dependence. Strong micro-location analysis still matters, especially around newer development corridors.

For expatriates, Bucaramanga offers a practical lifestyle with good healthcare, shopping, and regional access. It is often best suited to long-stay living or traditional rental strategies rather than aggressive short-term rental underwriting.

regulations.airbnbSummary: Nightly rentals are not the city's dominant strategy and should be treated cautiously. Building approval and registration still matter, and many investors prefer conventional leases.
regulations.airbnbDetails: If short-term rentals are part of the plan, confirm building policy in writing and review whether the target area genuinely supports visitor demand rather than assuming a tourism premium that may not exist.
regulations.airbnbSummaryEs: La corta estancia depende de reglamento del edificio y cumplimiento regulatorio local; no se debe asumir por demanda.
regulations.airbnbDetailsEs: Antes de comprar, valida licencias, régimen de propiedad horizontal y política de administración para evitar fricciones operativas.

cities.foreignOwnership: Foreign buyers face no general nationality-based restriction on urban property ownership in Bucaramanga. Standard title diligence and foreign investment registration still apply.
cities.mortgageNote: Foreign mortgage access is possible but limited; lower-leverage structures remain the prudent assumption in underwriting.

rentalMarket.headline: Bucaramanga is usually a local long-stay and family market with strong healthcare-related appeal and lighter speculation risk.
rentalMarket.tenantMix: Families, healthcare users, professionals, retirees, and returning Colombians.
rentalMarket.longStayDemand: Stable in Cabecera, Cacique, Sotomayor, and established service corridors.
rentalMarket.furnishedDemand: Selective, strongest for medical, executive, or transition rentals.
rentalMarket.shortStayReality: Usually a weak primary thesis compared with cleaner long-stay demand.
rentalMarket.landlordWatchouts: Do not import Medellin or coastal rent assumptions into a more local market.
rentalMarket.headlineEs: El arriendo en Bucaramanga se explica mejor por barrio, perfil de inquilino y reglas del edificio que por un titular de rentabilidad.
rentalMarket.longStayDemandEs: La larga estancia suele dar la base más estable de ocupación y caja.
rentalMarket.furnishedDemandEs: La demanda amoblada existe, pero su profundidad cambia por microzona y ticket mensual.
rentalMarket.shortStayRealityEs: La corta estancia exige verificación estricta de reglas, operación y estacionalidad.
rentalMarket.tenantMixEs: Mezcla de demanda local, ejecutiva, expatriada y de retorno colombiano según barrio.
rentalMarket.landlordWatchoutsEs: Controla vacancia, mantenimiento y costos ocultos para proteger flujo neto real.

cities.taglineEs: Orderly Mid-Sized City With Quality-Of-Life Appeal en Colombia
cities.shortTaglineEs: Bucaramanga: mercado activo, lectura por barrios y decisiones con datos locales.
cities.climateEs: Clima de Bucaramanga con variaciones de microzona que conviene validar durante visitas de mañana y tarde.
cities.paceEs: Ritmo urbano de Bucaramanga que cambia por barrio, movilidad diaria y tipo de comprador.
cities.historicalContextEs: Bucaramanga evolucionó con identidad regional propia y hoy combina capas históricas, servicios urbanos y submercados residenciales con comportamientos distintos por zona.
cities.investorAppealEs: El atractivo inversionista de Bucaramanga se apoya en demanda de arriendo segmentada, profundidad operativa y posibilidad de entrar por barrio con disciplina de precio por m2.
cities.lifestyleHighlightsEs: Compradores y familias valoran en Bucaramanga la combinación entre servicios, conectividad, salud y estilo de vida, siempre que la selección de barrio sea precisa.
introTextEs: Bucaramanga no se entiende solo con promedios de ciudad. La lectura correcta empieza por microzonas, caminabilidad real y calidad de servicios cotidianos.
Para compradores internacionales, locales y brokers, este mercado premia la disciplina en precio por m2, revisión de reglamentos y análisis de costos de mantenimiento antes de negociar.
La decisión más fuerte en Bucaramanga suele venir de combinar narrativa de vida diaria con números: salud, movilidad, carga de reparaciones y demanda de arriendo por barrio.
destinationHighlightsEs: Escena local y ritmo urbano con identidad propia.
Submercados por barrio con perfiles de comprador diferentes.
Conectividad y servicios que impactan la calidad de vida diaria.
watchoutsEs: Nunca extrapoles un barrio al resto de la ciudad.
Verifica reglamento de propiedad horizontal antes de modelar corta estancia.
Incluye mantenimiento y administración en la proyección desde el inicio.
first30DaysGuideEs: Camina el barrio en distintos horarios antes de comprometerte.
Valida reglamentos del edificio, reservas y estado de áreas comunes.
Alinea estrategia legal, migratoria y tributaria antes de cerrar.
cities.foreignOwnershipEs: La propiedad por extranjeros es posible, pero requiere documentación limpia, trazabilidad bancaria y revisión legal local.
cities.mortgageNoteEs: El apalancamiento para no residentes suele tener condiciones más exigentes; compara costo total y tiempos de aprobación.
